reasoning: This scenario is set in a simulated 2026 timeline. A synthetic market had a probability of 0.63 on April 2 for a U.S. strike on Yemen by April 30. The simulated news details that Houthis resumed strikes on Israel on March 28, 2026, and a U.S. carrier/forces (USS Tripoli) arrived in the region amidst soaring tensions with Iran and its proxies. Although tensions remain high and a retaliatory strike is plausible, 10 days have passed since the April 2 market estimate without an overt US strike, so the probability marginally decreases due to time decay (with only 18 days left). Minor, potentially unconfirmed anti-AQAP strikes were reported earlier in the year, but major DoD/news confirmation appears lacking. Thus, I estimate ~61% chance of a confirmed U.S. strike on Yemen by April 30. The "N, /, A" dates appear to be a parsing artifact of "N/A", so matching probabilities are supplied.
